item.title: Ti6Al4V DMLS: Aerospace and Medical Applications Guide
item.description: Ti6Al4V DMLS is the most-certified metal additive manufacturing combination in high-stakes industries. This guide covers design, post-processing, and qualification requirements for aerospace and medical applications.

item.title: About Titanium 3D Print
item.description:
Titanium Additive Manufacturing

Titanium 3D Print covers Ti6Al4V DMLS — the material and process combination that has done more to advance metal additive manufacturing adoption in aerospace and medical than any other.

Ti6Al4V’s combination of high strength (993–1,055 MPa), low density (4.43 g/cm³), corrosion resistance, and biocompatibility makes it irreplaceable. DMLS printing adds: near-net-shape complexity, 90%+ material efficiency versus machined billet, and lead time compression from 6–12 weeks (machined) to 5–10 business days.

item.title: Advertise on Titanium 3D Print
item.description:
Advertise on Titanium 3D Print

Titanium 3D Print reaches engineers and procurement managers actively sourcing metal additive manufacturing services, materials, and equipment.

Advertising Packages

Package Rate Placement
Banner Ad (728×90) $500/month Homepage
Sponsored Content $1,200/month Article feature
Network Package $3,500/month All PrintWire metal sites

Contact: maxdavisgroup@gmail.com

item.title: Services
item.description:
Ti6Al4V DMLS Parts

Ti6Al4V (Grade 5) is the workhorse titanium alloy for DMLS — the highest strength-to-weight ratio material in the additive manufacturing catalog. 993–1,055 MPa UTS at 4.43 g/cm³. 30% lighter than stainless steel at comparable strength. Certified to AMS 4999 (aerospace) and ASTM F136 (medical, using ELI grade). Build envelopes to 400 × 400 × 450 mm. Stress relief and optional HIP available.

Medical Titanium Implants

Ti6Al4V ELI (extra-low interstitial, Grade 23) for implantable devices. DMLS enables patient-specific geometry from CT/MRI data, porous lattice surfaces (40–80% porosity, 400–700 µm pore size) for bone ingrowth, and complex internal channels for vascular access devices. ISO 13485 quality management. FDA 510(k) technical file documentation available.
